headline: Silk Road
description: The Silk Road was a network of trade routes connecting East Asia to Europe from ~130 BCE to 1453 CE. It transported not just silk, but ideas, technologies, religions (Buddhism, Islam, Christianity), and diseases (Black Death). Not a single road — a web of land and sea routes across Eurasia.

citation.name: The Silk Roads: A New History of the World (Peter Frankopan)
